And all of a sudden from one meeting to the next from one call to the next They're like the only one who hasn't done anything. Well, that was nor that was acceptable behavior in the group They were in before now. It's clearly unacceptable behavior. And in many cases that like jars them into action but For the most part by the time we get them either some ingrained level of Do it don't do it and see I don't first of all, I don't think we ever actually we haven't seen 80 20 rule In the 515 80 and changed in at least 50 years that I know of right so all of us Then all of the books and the tapes and the CDs and all of this stuff I mean look 20 years ago. You can find a self-help book now you go to Barnes and Oboots You don't have to go to bookstores on the friggin Internet you Oprah's doing it for God's sakes I mean all you had to do is watch Oprah, you know, you have to get off a couch if you're like comes to you It's everywhere and all this technology that's made it easier So I mean, you know like there's faxes and emails and all this the percentages haven't changed one damn wit Follow any P any group a hundred people for 40 years to age 65 1% rich 4% financially secure 15% have to keep working to survive 80% or either dead or broke and change First time I saw Social Security Administration stats for the 1963 the last ones I saw from 1993 no change think of everything is changing between handshakes damn thing So what I say the population keeps reordering itself all the time into 515 80 it does so even a coach a group You got 20 in a coaching group it reorders itself into 5% of them are kicking ass and taking names 15% are like doing pretty well All right 80% are pretty much wondering how in the hell the 20% are doing it And so I don't think we changed that I think we facilitated to be faster easier better bigger we really help winners win earlier faster bigger we rarely change a Dedicated loser Into a winner now it may not require that however to delight them Okay, but we don't really do it and that's why See I once I had my amway experience the first like the big aha that came out of that for me Is I didn't want to be in the in a business where my? Compensation was determined by someone else's performance Right where where the compensation was based on turning chickens into eagles, you know, I mean I Got it like you know the blinding flash of the obvious You know I'm a 17 year old kid and this guy's a 42 year old Mature adult business owner He can't manage to show up at the opportunity meeting at his own house on time in order to let the guests in This tells me this is no way to try and make your money, you know So see our business is all based on consumption Not performance Performance happens. That's great. I mean but otherwise we're based on consumption you buy it you take it home You come to the seminar you go home do something don't do something don't make any difference I got the same amount of money Every once in a while I think gee it'd be great to get some more money from the ones who do something But in order to do that I'd have to change the whole formula and I like that right So I don't think we make winners out of anybody I think we help winners win bigger faster Easier that's what I think we do Craig Then I have a situation where I've had some of my guys nicking bits and pieces of my coffee and Pretty much my mindset has been if they're a client. They've paid me a lot of money I'm sort of tolerant of it if they're in my coaching You know tolerant and I wanted to know your mindset on that or your tolerance of threshold because now I'm seeing guys who are like Newsletter subscribers Nick and the whole newsletter letter, you know my newsletter for their newsletter and I have a that's not I'm not tolerant of that at all It's a very difficult part of this business and First of all, it's very hard to define actually what's Fair use and what isn't fair use it's sort of like art or pornography, you know You know it when you see it But explaining it in advance to somebody is a little tough, right?
